question: Copy-on-Write (CoW) vs Merge-on-Read (MoR) in Iceberg: what are the tradeoffs?
answer: Copy-on-Write rewrites entire data files when any row changes, so reads are always fast (no merge overhead) but writes are expensive because large files must be rewritten for small updates. Merge-on-Read writes lightweight delete files or position delete files alongside the original data files, making writes fast but requiring readers to apply the delete files at query time, adding latency. CoW is best for read-heavy tables with infrequent updates, such as slowly changing dimensions. MoR is best for write-heavy or streaming tables where updates arrive constantly, such as CDC pipelines or event deduplication. Compaction (OPTIMIZE) converts MoR tables back to clean CoW state on a schedule.
